Notes: TURNKEY BUILD — JLCPCB-assembled PCB with pre-built modules.
Goal: minimize soldering. Only connections are plug-in connectors.
Estimated total cost: ~$25-40 for 5 assembled boards (with JLCPCB SMT assembly)
See docs/JLCPCB_ORDERING_GUIDE.md for step-by-step ordering instructions.

Notes: ~$1-2. Set output to ~12V with trim pot before connecting. Plugs into JST header on PCB.
v3.1: rechargeable 1S LiPo pouch cell. 103450 = ~52x34x10 mm fits the enclosure bay. Must have a JST-PH pigtail — if not wired, solder one on carefully. $8-12.
v3.1: USB-C LiPo charger with built-in DW01A protection (over-charge, over-discharge, over-current). ~$0.50 ea in 15-pack. Mount on internal shelf in the battery bay.
Standard USB-C cable. Plugs into the TP4056 module for charging. ~$0 (you have one)

JLCPCB P/N or Source: (A) Amazon LCD1602 I2C kit ~$3, or (B) Winstar WEH001602 + PCF8574 ~$25, or (C) Newhaven NHD-0216AW-IB3 ~$28
Notes: v3.1: 16x2 character display for status/battery/frequency. Option (A) is the cheap HD44780 LCD (LCD_IS_OLED=0). Options (B)(C) are character OLEDs with much nicer contrast and viewing angle (set LCD_IS_OLED=1). All three work with the same firmware driver.
Pre-made JST-XH pigtail cable. Connects PCB to cup adapter. ~$1 for a 5-pack.
Short JST-PH pigtail to go between TP4056 BAT pads and the PCB BAT IN header. ~$1 for a 10-pack. Many LiPo cells already ship with one attached.
4-pin female-female Dupont jumper (VCC/GND/SDA/SCL), ~100mm. Connects the lid LCD to the PCB LCD header. ~$0.50.
